de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext

Class ClassFileDeclarationBuilder

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext.ClassFileDeclarationBuilder
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<ClassFileDeclarationBuilder>

    public class ClassFileDeclarationBuilder
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<ClassFileDeclarationBuilder>
    Make a ClassDeclaration out of a class file. Given a ClassFile read in via recoder create a corresponding ClassDeclaration to be registered in the system instead. This allows to rely on one mechanism of Recoder-KeY correspondance. The information from the classfile are not retrieved via the various services but via direct queries. This makes sure that the classfile is not known to a classfile repository and saves space. Method bodies cannot be retrieved so that all methods/constructors have got a "null-body", the resulting declaration is a stub. The information is stored into a compilation unit within the appropriate package. TODO Recoder does not allow to detect if an inner class is static or not because it discards the information about inner classes all to early. That is why all inner classes are non-static. Does this matter? Patch is on the way TODO inner classes are not detected to be "private". A combination of private and static seems impossible with recoder TODO Possible improvement: Remove "synthetic" members (JVM spec chapter 4)
    See Also:
    ClassFileDeclarationManager

      create a new ClassDeclaration builder.

      is the considered ClassFile the representation of an anymous class or a class declared within a program? This is the case if the last $ is followed by a digit.

      is the considered ClassFile the byte code of an inner class? This is done checking the fully qualified class name.

      set the location to be stored in the compilation unit, mainly for error reporting.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • ClassFileDeclarationBuilder

        public ClassFileDeclarationBuilder(ClassFileDeclarationManager manager,
                                   ClassFile classFile)
        create a new ClassDeclaration builder. The builder can be used to create a ClassDeclaration for a single class file.
        Parameters:
        classFile - class file to be investigated
        manager - the manager to which this builder belongs

    • Method Detail

      • getFullClassname

        public java.lang.String getFullClassname()
        get the class name stored in the class file. May contain '$' for inner or anonymous classes
        Returns:
        the physical name of the class in the class file

      • getClassName

        public java.lang.String getClassName()
        get the class name of this class. If it is an inner class, it is only the part after $, or the complete short name otherwise. Anonymous classes are therefore referenced by "EnclosingClass$11" for instance.
        Returns:
        the name of the class

      • makeCompilationUnit

        public CompilationUnit makeCompilationUnit()
        retrieve the compilation unit for the class file under consideration. The second and following calls will return the cached value of the initial calculation. This method calls makeTypeDeclaration() and embeds this type into a compilation unit.
        Returns:
        a compilation unit corresponding to the class file.

      • makeTypeDeclaration

        public TypeDeclaration makeTypeDeclaration()
        retrieve a TypeDeclaration for the class file under consideration The second and following calls will return the cached value of the initial calculation.
        Returns:
        a TypeDeclaration corresponding to the class file

      • setDataLocation

        public void setDataLocation(DataLocation dataLocation)
        set the location to be stored in the compilation unit, mainly for error reporting.
        Parameters:
        dataLocation - the DataLocation to be set or null

      • isInnerClass

        public boolean isInnerClass()
        is the considered ClassFile the byte code of an inner class? This is done checking the fully qualified class name. Does it contain a "$" and is this character not followed by a number
        Returns:
        true iff the classFile under inspection is an inner class

      • isAnonymousClass

        public boolean isAnonymousClass()
        is the considered ClassFile the representation of an anymous class or a class declared within a program? This is the case if the last $ is followed by a digit.
        Returns:
        true iff the classFile under inspection is an anon. or in-code-class

      • attachToEnclosingDeclaration

        public void attachToEnclosingDeclaration()
        If this is a builder for an inner class, the declaration has to be attached to the enclosing class. This method adds the resulting declaration to an existing type declaration. A reference to the enclosing builder is stored to retrieve the type parameter information, e.g.

      • getEnclosingName

        public java.lang.String getEnclosingName()
        get the fully qualified name of the enclosing class of an inner class
        Returns:
        class name, not null

      • makeEmptyClassDeclaration

        public static CompilationUnit makeEmptyClassDeclaration(ProgramFactory programFactory,
                                                        java.lang.String fullClassName)
                                                 throws ParserException
        make a stub class declaration for a fully qualified type reference. If the type reference stands for an array, the trailing [] are discarded first.
        Parameters:
        programFactory - factory to use as parser
        fullClassName - the fully qualified type name
        Returns:
        a compilation unit that has not been added to a source repository yet
        Throws:
        ParserException - thrown by the parser

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object

      • compareTo

        public int compareTo(ClassFileDeclarationBuilder o)
        compare to class file declaration builders. comparison is performed upon the full classnames
        Specified by:
        compareTo in interface java.lang.Comparable<ClassFileDeclarationBuilder>

        Overrides:

        Overrides:
